page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the N1 family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz, consisting of 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU configuration includes a 45 MB L3 cache, 32 KB L1d cache, and 0.25 MB L2 cache. It supports AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA instruction sets, but lacks AVX-512 extensions. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 0.9 GB per vCPU. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark results position this instance in the lower performance tiers. In stress-ng testing, the single-core CPU performance scored 1125.48 and multi-core scored 2941.66, both falling into the bottom 25% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth benchmarks also reflect weak performance, with cached read speeds at 162.49 GB/sec and uncached write speeds at 21.69 GB/sec. Geekbench scores further confirm this trend, with a single-core score of 926.0 and a multi-core score of 1979.0. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as Gzip compression at 18.99 GB/sec and Passmark encryption at 1338.88, are similarly constrained.

From a resource density and cost-efficiency perspective, the n1-highcpu-4 offers a budget-friendly entry point for compute-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints. However, the low memory allocation of 1 GB per vCPU represents a significant tradeoff, limiting its suitability for memory-intensive applications. The instance is best suited for lightweight, compute-oriented workloads such as basic web serving, simple software compilation, and low-intensity batch processing.

Memory bandwidth is similarly limited, with uncached write speeds of 21.69 GB/sec. While cost-effective for basic processing tasks due to its low resource footprint, the limited memory capacity represents a clear tradeoff. It is best suited for lightweight, compute-oriented workloads such as basic web serving, simple software compilation, and low-intensity batch processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 server featuring 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 4 GB of RAM. It offers a budget-friendly, low-memory configuration suited for basic web serving and light compute tasks.
